scenario_text: Caller claims to be a CBI officer, says my Aadhaar card was used in a money laundering case, and tells me not to disconnect the call or I will be arrested immediately.
red_flags: Claims to be CBI/police over phone call
Demands victim stay on call continuously
guidance: Real police/CBI never arrest, investigate, or issue notices over a phone or video call. They do not ask you to stay on a call. Hang up immediately and verify by calling the local police station directly.

scenario_text: A person on video call in a police-style uniform says a parcel with my ID was intercepted at customs containing drugs, and I need to pay a fine to avoid arrest.
guidance: Customs and courier companies never contact individuals demanding payment to avoid arrest. This is a scripted digital arrest scam. Do not pay. End the call and report to cybercrime.gov.in.

scenario_text: Someone claiming to be from the Enforcement Directorate says my bank account is linked to a money laundering investigation and I must transfer funds to a 'safe government account' for verification.
red_flags: Claims funds must be moved to a 'safe account'
guidance: No government agency will ever ask you to transfer money to a 'safe account' for verification. This is the single most common digital arrest scam tactic. Never transfer funds. Report immediately.

scenario_text: The caller insists I keep my camera on for hours, watches me continuously, and says I cannot leave the room or contact anyone until the 'investigation' is complete.
guidance: This is a hostage-style psychological manipulation tactic used in digital arrest scams. No legal process requires continuous video monitoring by phone. Disconnect and contact a trusted family member immediately.

scenario_text: I received a message with a scanned document that looks like a court summons, saying I have to respond within 2 hours or a warrant will be issued.
red_flags: Fake scanned legal document sent via message
No official government letterhead verification possible
guidance: Real legal summons are delivered through official channels with proper time for response, never demanding action within hours over chat. Verify independently through the court's official website.

scenario_text: I got a call saying my FedEx parcel was seized because it contains illegal items, and I need to pay a fee or share my bank details to release it.
red_flags: Uses courier company name as cover
guidance: Courier companies do not call demanding fees for seized illegal parcels. Never share bank details over the phone. Verify directly on the courier company's official app or website.

scenario_text: I received an SMS saying my bank KYC will expire today and I must click a link and enter my account details immediately to avoid account suspension.
red_flags: Requests full account details via link
guidance: Banks never ask you to update KYC via SMS links. Do not click the link. Visit your bank branch or official app directly to verify KYC status.

scenario_text: A caller claiming to be from my bank's fraud department says suspicious activity was found on my card and asks me to share the OTP to 'block' the card.
red_flags: Claims to be bank fraud department
OTP request is the key red flag
guidance: Banks never ask for your OTP over a call, under any circumstance, including to 'block' a card. Never share OTP with anyone. Hang up and call your bank's official number directly.

scenario_text: I took a small loan from an app, and now they are calling my contacts and threatening to morph my photos unless I pay much more than I borrowed.
red_flags: Demands payment far exceeding loan amount
guidance: This is loan app extortion, a recognized cybercrime pattern. Do not engage further payments. Screenshot all threats and report to cybercrime.gov.in and local police immediately.

scenario_text: Someone added me to a WhatsApp group promising guaranteed daily returns on stock trading, and asked me to deposit money into a personal account to start.
red_flags: Deposit to personal/individual account, not a registered broker
Pressure to invest more after initial small 'profit'
guidance: No legitimate investment guarantees fixed daily returns. Never transfer funds to a personal account for trading. Verify any broker's SEBI registration before investing.

scenario_text: A stranger video-called me, the call turned inappropriate, and now they are threatening to share a recording with my contacts unless I pay them.
guidance: Do not pay — payment rarely stops the threats and can encourage further extortion. Block the contact, do not delete evidence, and report immediately to cybercrime.gov.in's dedicated section for such cases.

scenario_text: I got a message offering a work-from-home job with high pay for simple tasks like liking videos, but they ask for a small 'registration fee' upfront.
red_flags: Unrealistically high pay for simple tasks
guidance: Legitimate employers never ask candidates to pay money to get a job. Do not pay any fee. Verify the company's official website and registration before proceeding.

scenario_text: Someone called claiming to help me receive a refund, and asked me to open my UPI app and enter a code they read out, or approve a request on my phone.
red_flags: Asks victim to approve a UPI collect request
Reads out a code to 'complete' refund
guidance: Refunds never require you to enter a code or approve a request in your UPI app — that action actually sends money out, not receives it. Never approve unfamiliar UPI requests. Decline and report.

scenario_text: I've been chatting online with someone for weeks who says they love me, and now they're stuck abroad and need money urgently for a medical emergency or customs fee.
red_flags: Relationship built quickly online, never met in person
Avoids video calls or in-person meetings
guidance: This is a classic romance scam pattern. Do not send money to someone you have never met in person, regardless of the emotional story. Verify identity independently before any financial commitment.

scenario_text: I received a message saying I won a lottery or lucky draw worth lakhs of rupees, and I need to pay a processing fee or tax to claim it.
red_flags: Requests upfront processing fee or tax
No prior entry into any such lottery
guidance: You cannot win a lottery you never entered. Legitimate prizes never require upfront payment to release winnings. Do not pay any amount. Ignore and block the sender.

scenario_text: My phone suddenly lost network signal for hours, and later I found unauthorized transactions on my bank account that I never approved.
red_flags: Sudden unexplained loss of mobile network
Unauthorized bank transactions following signal loss
Possible prior phishing that leaked personal details
Common precursor to large-scale account takeover
guidance: This pattern strongly suggests SIM swap fraud. Immediately contact your telecom provider and bank to freeze accounts and reissue your SIM securely. File a police complaint right away.

scenario_text: I got a call saying I'm eligible for a government subsidy or PM scheme benefit, and they need my bank account and Aadhaar details to process it immediately.
red_flags: Unsolicited call about government scheme eligibility
Requests Aadhaar and bank details over phone
No official government verification channel used
guidance: Government schemes never require you to share bank/Aadhaar details over an unsolicited call. Verify any scheme only through official government portals like india.gov.in.

scenario_text: I received a message saying my electricity connection will be disconnected tonight due to unpaid bill, and I must call a number or click a link to pay immediately.
red_flags: Unofficial payment link or number provided
guidance: Utility companies provide advance notice through official bills/apps, not last-minute threatening messages with external links. Verify your bill status only through the official utility website or app.

scenario_text: My bank's relationship manager called from a number saved in my contacts to inform me about a new savings scheme, without asking for OTP or passwords.
guidance: This appears to be a routine, low-risk interaction. Legitimate bank staff will never ask for OTP, PIN, or full card details, even in genuine calls. Always verify independently if unsure.

scenario_text: I received a tracking update SMS from Amazon with a link that matched the official Amazon tracking domain, confirming my order is out for delivery.
guidance: This looks like a standard, low-risk delivery notification. Always double check that links match the company's official domain before clicking, even when it appears legitimate.
